agent_notes: Havering is PAINTS-useful but not volume-rich: the borough has a strong outer-London micropub/heritage spine, yet very few qualifying independent bookshop-cafes, curated markets, or gallery-density backups.

No Creative Walks were included because Havering parks and routes found during research, including Hornchurch Country Park, Ingrebourne Valley, Bedfords Park, Raphael Park and Rainham Marshes, do not appear on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list.

Many otherwise attractive picks are Tier B because the same-category backup rule is hard to satisfy in a dispersed borough; host must verify Google rating/review counts, group walk-in tolerance, and any private-hire conflicts before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site and CAMRA list Monday closed; Tuesday-Wednesday 16:00-22:00; Thursday-Friday 14:00-22:30; Saturday 12:00-22:30; Sunday 12:00-21:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank holiday/private-event exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says no booking and no time limit; group of 8-12 walk-in seating is still UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for the intended slot.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-10 off-peak; 12 may need an early arrival or advance heads-up because it is a micropub-scale room.
venues.capacity_notes: Compact independent micropub; best treated as conversational standing/seated mix rather than a reserved-table dinner format.
venues.why_fits_paints: Hornchurch's first micropub has a community-first, cask-and-cider culture that gives strangers something concrete to talk about. It is intimate, locally loved and more characterful than a high-street bar.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue has CAMRA validation, including London Pub of the Year and UK Cider Pub of the Year signals in its own social bio, which turns the drinks list into a local-pride story rather than just a beverage menu.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Changing real ales, cider, craft beer, wine and spirits.
venues.safety_notes: Town-centre route; use well-lit North Street/High Street corridors and avoid late-night dispersal toward quieter side streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6-8 min walk via North Street and High Street - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Micropub capacity and Friday/Saturday peak crowding; group walk-in must be confirmed despite the no-booking signal.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram, X and London CAMRA list Monday-Tuesday 16:00-23:00 and Wednesday-Sunday 12:00-23:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank holiday/private-event exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram states walk-in only; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely viable off-peak; small-room micropub means 12 should arrive early and be prepared to split conversationally.
venues.capacity_notes: Micropub with garden/accessibility signals from CAMRA; exact seating capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 18:00-20:00; Sunday early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: A pure neighbourhood micropub with real ale, cider, craft beer and cocktails, plus a local CAMRA award trail. It gives Havering a strong drinks venue outside the Romford/Hornchurch obvious axis.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue opened in 2017 and has been listed by CAMRA with local Pub of the Year signals, making it part of the borough's modern micropub movement.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale, craft beer, cider, wine, spirits and cocktails.
venues.safety_notes: Main Road is a lit suburban corridor; route attendees by Gidea Park station and main roads after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk via Main Road/Squirrels Heath Lane - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Walk-in only helps operationally but does not guarantee a single area for 8-12; peak evenings may be too tight.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Monday-Friday 16:00-23:00; Saturday 12:00-23:00; Sunday 12:00-22:30, with bank-holiday notes. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with venue social/phone.
venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub format suggests walk-ins; group of 8-12 seating/walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for 8-10 off-peak; 12 likely fragile unless the courtyard/outside area is available.
venues.capacity_notes: Pubsgalore review describes a cramped rectangular micropub with a garden/yard; capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: A locally focused micropub with real ale, cider, beer-festival energy and a tucked-away station-neighbourhood feel. Strong PAINTS texture, but the backup rule is weaker in Upminster.
venues.anecdote_story: Micropub reports describe it as a former office converted into a taproom that opened in 2015, part of the outer-east London micropub wave.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Changing cask ales, local beer, cider, craft keg and pub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Upminster station; use main lit routes and avoid dispersal through residential side streets late.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying independent drinks backup found within 10 min - closest plausible alternatives are more conventional pubs or chain-led venues that do not meet PAINTS drinks criteria.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight PAINTS-coded same-category backup; group seating is unverified.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists daily opening from noon, with Monday-Tuesday to midnight and other day details on listing. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current hours directly.
venues.walk_in_policy: Traditional pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable for a traditional pub table/side area if confirmed in advance.
venues.capacity_notes: Late-19th-century local-heritage-interest pub per CAMRA; exact group capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: Not as drinks-led as a micropub, but it offers an older suburban pub texture and works as a softer backup/alternative around Gidea Park.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA records the building as a late-19th-century pub and a London Borough of Havering Building of Local Heritage Interest.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks; cask status should be host-confirmed because CAMRA currently flags cask ale as not available.
venues.safety_notes: Residential suburban setting; route from Gidea Park station via main roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: May feel more standard local pub than PAINTS drinks special; current drinks quality and Google rating/review count need confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Greene King and CAMRA list Monday-Thursday 11:30-23:00; Friday 11:30-midnight; Saturday 11:00-midnight; Sunday 11:00-22:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event/private-hire conflicts.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub accepts ordinary walk-ins; bookable tables also available. Group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely physically suitable, especially around early evening folk-session energy, but less intimate than a micropub.
venues.capacity_notes: Large historic town-centre pub; group seating likely possible but should be confirmed.
venues.best_time_slot: Monday 18:30-20:30 during/around the open folk session, or Thursday 17:30-19:30.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Grade II town-centre pub with a live folk-session layer gives Romford a story-rich drinks option. It is chain-owned, so it cannot score like an independent PAINTS bar, but the building and music culture rescue it.
venues.anecdote_story: Romford Ceilidh Band lists weekly 2026 open folk sessions at the Golden Lion, with tunes and pub singing in the front downstairs bar.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks and food; live folk-session culture on Mondays.
venues.safety_notes: Central Romford is busy and transit-rich but can be rowdy late; use early evening slots and group departures to station.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying independent drinks backup found within 10 min - nearby pubs are mostly chain-led or insufficiently PAINTS-coded.
venues.risks_watchouts: Greene King ownership and town-centre atmosphere make it less off-grid; host must verify current Google rating/review count and event-night noise.

venues.opening_hours: Havering Council says open Wednesday, Friday and Saturday; Romford Market site says 09:00-16:30, 17:00 on Saturday, regardless of weather. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank holiday/special-event variations.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access public market; walk-in is inherent.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a loose browsing market day, not a single-table conversation format.
venues.capacity_notes: Large outdoor market place with changing stalls; meeting point should be outside main flows.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:30-12:30 for best atmosphere, or Friday 10:30-12:00 for easier movement.
venues.why_fits_paints: The borough's strongest market candidate is historically deep rather than boutique: the market right dates to 1247 and still anchors Romford's civic identity. It is Tier B because the stall mix is general rather than producer-curated.
venues.anecdote_story: Romford's market right was established by royal order in 1247, originally around livestock and everyday trade, and the market remains a visible piece of the old Liberty of Havering story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Outdoor market stalls including food, clothes, books, produce and general goods; exact trader mix varies by day.
venues.safety_notes: Busy town-centre setting close to station; avoid late afternoon dispersal after market close.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2-4 min walk across Market Place - indoor backup for weather and stall weakness.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not consistently curated; host should scout the intended market day to confirm food/craft density and meeting point.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Monday-Wednesday 09:00-17:00; Thursday-Saturday 09:00-19:00; Sunday and bank holidays 10:00-16:00, with individual shop variations.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access indoor shopping hall; walk-in is inherent, but individual traders may vary.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a weather-proof browsing stop for 8-12, especially paired with Romford Market.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor hall with independent traders and food outlets; exact group gathering area is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.why_fits_paints: The hall is not polished, but it has useful market-day texture: independent traders, food outlets, local businesses and a long-standing position beside the historic market.
venues.anecdote_story: Its own site describes over a century of traders and an independent fabric of shops, food, jewellery, gifts and local services in the heart of Romford.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent retail stalls/shops, cafes and food outlets; trader mix varies.
venues.safety_notes: Indoor town-centre environment; use as poor-weather fallback and keep meeting point inside a visible entrance.
venues.risks_watchouts: More indoor-shopping-hall than curated market; some individual trader hours differ from hall hours.

venues.opening_hours: Havering events listing says each Saturday from 11 April 2026 to 26 September 2026 there is a gate sale of excess produce provided by plot holders. Exact time is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open community gate sale implied; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as a short local-produce stop for a small group, not a full 2-3 hour market event by itself.
venues.capacity_notes: Allotment-gate sale; likely small and weather-dependent.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday morning, after confirming the exact sale hours and plot-holder turnout.
venues.why_fits_paints: A modest but genuinely local producer signal: excess allotment produce sold at the gate, offering a borough-specific, human-scale alternative to generic food shopping.
venues.anecdote_story: The appeal is the micro-economy of plot holders sharing surplus produce: a small civic ritual rather than a commercial market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal allotment surplus produce; exact stall mix varies.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; route by main residential streets and avoid lingering after sale close.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying market/craft backup found within 15 min walk or transit - Romford Market is the closest robust market but exceeds the PAINTS relaxed market backup radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small scale, weather dependence and unverified hours make this publishable only after a host scout/call.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Wednesday-Friday 11:00-16:30, last gallery entry 15:30; Saturdays only when events are taking place and times vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm volunteer closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public museum admission; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a quiet small-group daytime visit if the museum confirms staffing/capacity.
venues.capacity_notes: Small local museum; temporary exhibition access may be affected by scheduled events.
venues.why_fits_paints: A borough-history museum in a former brewery building gives PAINTS a direct Havering story: market town, suburban growth, local memory and community collecting.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum bills itself as the hidden gem of the Greater London borough and runs reminiscence/history programming, including July 2026 events such as Havering in Bloom and a Highgate Cemetery talk.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent local-history galleries and event-linked temporary display space; exact current exhibition must be confirmed because access depends on the event programme.
venues.safety_notes: Central Romford daytime visit; route attendees directly from station/high street.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Romford Shopping Hall is close but market/retail rather than museum/gallery.
venues.risks_watchouts: Volunteer-run hours and temporary-exhibition access are fragile; host must confirm the live display before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: National Trust/Instagram signals: garden and cafe Wednesday-Sunday 11:00-16:00; Hall Thursday-Saturday 11:00-16:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact date ticket availability.
venues.walk_in_policy: National Trust ticketing/visit booking exists; garden/cafe are more drop-in. Treat Hall entry for 8-12 as UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a quiet 8-12 person heritage/gallery day if entry capacity is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Small Georgian house, community garden, cafe, Hayloft and second-hand bookshop; timed/limited house capacity may apply.
venues.why_fits_paints: Rainham Hall is Havering's most elegant PAINTS heritage pick: Georgian merchant-house story, community garden, changing exhibitions and a small bookshop/cafe ecosystem. It would otherwise be Tier A, but no same-category backup exists within 10 minutes.
venues.anecdote_story: Built in 1729 for Captain John Harle, the hall is an unusually preserved early Georgian merchant's house and has even served as a filming location.
venues.serve_or_on_view: National Trust house displays, changing exhibitions, Secret Artist/other community exhibition programming when scheduled, garden, cafe and second-hand bookshop.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime slots; Rainham village centre is transit-accessible but group should depart together toward station/bus stops.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - RAF Hornchurch and Havering Museum are culturally strong but outside the strict walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: House opening is more limited than garden/cafe; host must confirm live exhibition and booking/walk-in handling.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Saturday-Sunday 11:00-16:00; weekdays for group/school bookings. Events calendar lists Public Opening 2026 dates with normal admission and no advanced booking required. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Weekend public openings say advanced booking is not required; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a small guided/quiet heritage group if the centre confirms staffing and tea-room capacity.
venues.capacity_notes: Volunteer heritage centre with tea room open weekends 11:00-16:00; group capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: A focused, volunteer-run local museum about Hornchurch Aerodrome gives the borough a precise wartime-memory anchor without the generic feel of a big central museum.
venues.anecdote_story: The centre opened in 2021 and covers the aerodrome's history from 1915 to closure in 1962, including Battle of Britain connections and personal stories.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Artefacts, photographs, storyboards, aircraft/squadron history and tea room.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime/weekend only; Suttons Lane route should be pre-checked for solo arrivals, especially in winter dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weekend-only public opening and volunteer operation; verify live opening and group handling.

venues.opening_hours: Live Well Havering lists Monday-Friday 09:00-22:00; Saturday 09:00-16:00; Sunday 09:30-13:30. Fry's Gallery exhibition access and current show times are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery/exhibition access appears public when open, but group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a short gallery/craft-centre visit if the current exhibition is open and not displaced by classes.
venues.capacity_notes: Community arts centre with rooms/classes; gallery capacity and current exhibition access should be checked by phone.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning or Saturday 11:00-13:00 after confirming gallery access.
venues.why_fits_paints: Fairkytes is a local art-making hub rather than a destination gallery, which makes it useful for PAINTS if a current Fry's Gallery show is strong.
venues.anecdote_story: Havering's arts pages point visitors to Fry's Gallery for exhibition information, while local listings show active classes and community art use.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fry's Gallery exhibitions and community arts activity; July 2026 Havering London listing included a mixed-media art exhibition at Fry's Gallery.
venues.safety_notes: Hornchurch town-centre setting; route from station/high street along lit streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibitions are not consistently surfaced on one official page; host must confirm what is actually on view.

venues.opening_hours: Official visit page lists 2025 open days April-October, usually 10:00-16:00; 2026 event listings show public open days such as July 11 and 12 with specific times. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 2026 public open day and ticketing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Visitor centre/gardens free on open days; windmill admission by ticket. Group of 8-12 walk-in/ticket availability is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good on an open day for a heritage object-led visit; less reliable as a standing event because dates are limited.
venues.capacity_notes: Windmill tour access may be capacity-limited and height/footwear restrictions may apply; visitor centre/gardens more flexible.
venues.why_fits_paints: A restored working smock mill gives PAINTS a vivid, photographable object and a strong local labour/volunteer story.
venues.anecdote_story: Built in 1803 and restored through a major volunteer-led effort, the windmill now has a visitor centre, gardens and interpretation around its machinery and Upminster's milling past.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Windmill, visitor centre, gardens, sails turning weather permitting on some open days.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; do not turn the surrounding field/gardens into a Creative Walk because the park is not on the PAINTS safety list.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Upminster Tithe Barn is closed/2026 dates to be confirmed and cannot be used as a backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited open days and ticketing make it operationally fragile; confirm before programming.

venues.opening_hours: Official food and drink page lists cafe Monday-Saturday 10:00-15:00; evening from one hour before main house performances to interval end. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm summer/holiday exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Cafe appears open to visitors during public hours; group of 8-12 walk-in seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a daytime literary/theatre-text conversation if cafe tables are available; not a true bookshop-cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Theatre foyer/cafe environment; capacity changes around performances and matinees.
venues.why_fits_paints: Havering lacks independent bookshop-cafes, so this is a theatre-texts-and-coffee compromise: a producing theatre with cafe, youth/community programme and enough story for a reading-led meet-up.
venues.anecdote_story: Queen's Theatre opened in 1953 and was rebuilt on Billet Lane in 1975; it is now a Grade II listed mid-scale producing theatre and a major Hornchurch cultural anchor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cafe drinks/cakes/lunch; QBar before performances; theatre programmes and live show context rather than bookshelves.
venues.safety_notes: Town-centre Hornchurch setting; use daytime cafe hours or performance nights with planned group departures.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying books-and-coffee backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying borough alternative is Rainham Hall Second-hand Bookshop & Stables Cafe by transit, outside PAINTS backup radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: This is a cultural cafe, not a bookshop; include only if PAINTS accepts theatre/literary-text hybrids for Havering.

venues.venue_name: Rainham Hall Second-hand Bookshop & Stables Cafe
venues.opening_hours: National Trust ticket page says garden and cafe Wednesday-Sunday and Hall Thursday-Saturday; Instagram says garden 11:00-16:00 and house Thursday-Saturday 11:00-16:00. Bookshop hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe likely drop-in when open; group of 8-12 seating and bookshop access are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially strong for a quiet books-and-coffee hour if the cafe can seat the group and the bookshop is open.
venues.capacity_notes: Small second-hand bookshop and Stables Cafe; capacity for one PAINTS group is likely limited.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the best Havering books-and-coffee compromise found: real pre-loved books, cafe, garden and heritage setting in one place.
venues.anecdote_story: The National Trust describes a small second-hand bookshop stocked with eclectic pre-loved books for adults and children, positioned within the Rainham Hall visitor welcome ecosystem.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Second-hand books, cafe drinks/food, garden and heritage setting.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; route arrivals from Rainham station/bus stops through village centre.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying books-and-coffee backup found within 10 min - Havering has no verified independent bookshop-cafe cluster nearby.
venues.risks_watchouts: Bookshop and cafe are small; current open status and group seating must be confirmed.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; cannot be sourced or used as a walk backup regardless of local appeal.
Strong nature/culture potential but not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list, so excluded.
Photogenic Romford park but absent from the PAINTS approved Creative Walk safety list; excluded.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; excluded despite landscape/story potential.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Upminster Tithe Barn Museum of Nostalgia
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Official site says 2026 open days are to be confirmed after closure/restoration works; not operationally robust enough for inclusion or backup.
Meets the literal books-plus-coffee pattern but is a national chain inside a retail setting, below PAINTS character threshold.
Operationally easy and close to Hornchurch backups, but chain-led/generic pub styling and Tripadvisor rating signals are below PAINTS drinks threshold.
Useful as a possible emergency Hornchurch drinks backup, but nightclub/bar positioning, late-night hours and weak PAINTS story make it below inclusion threshold.
Good local produce signal but it is a farm shop, not a market day with stalls/maker atmosphere; kept out to avoid padding.
